completed_tasks.name: 15-02 trigger-manager.js (6 condition kinds + AND/OR + edge/fire-once + ReDoS regex + cap, TRIG-02..07, LIFE-04)
15-03 SEAM wiring (evaluate() + atomic fired write-back) + background.js glue
Code review (1 BLOCKER CR-01 ReDoS bypass + 4 warnings) found and fixed
Phase verification (5/5 success criteria, 11/11 REQ-IDs, status passed)

human_actions_pending.action: Milestone-end Chrome MV3 live-fire / SW-eviction UAT for the trigger family
human_actions_pending.context: Phase 15 is unit-testable in isolation (element value injected as a test input); real-fire MV3-survival UAT is deferred to milestone close per 15-VALIDATION.md and the Phase-14 deferral pattern. NOT a Phase-15 blocker.

decisions.decision: ReDoS guard uses a paren-aware structural hasNestedQuantifier() detector, not just length caps
decisions.rationale: Code review (CR-01) proved exponential backtracking hangs on ~34 chars, far under the 10000-char text cap, so length caps cannot bound it; only rejecting the nested-quantifier pattern shape at compile time can. The original plan/research claim that length caps are the load-bearing ReDoS guarantee was FALSE for the exponential class.

decisions.decision: evaluate() is a pure function; the trigger-lifecycle SEAM owns the atomic fired write-back
decisions.rationale: D-02 — keeps exactly-one-fire-across-eviction correct and the engine unit-testable with no browser/storage.

decisions.decision: Concurrency cap active-count reads listArmedSnapshots() (storage), not an in-heap Set
decisions.rationale: D-09 — an in-heap counter resets to 0 on SW eviction and would silently stop enforcing the cap; _withArmLock serializes the check-then-arm (TOCTOU).

decisions.decision: Two documented accepted security residuals carried forward
decisions.rationale: T-15-03 (_sepCache growth, bounded by locale set) and the CR-01 residual (static ReDoS detection cannot be proven complete; a hard guarantee needs an off-thread killable match — offscreen + watchdog — tracked for a future phase).

uncommitted_files: .planning/config.json (pre-existing, out-of-scope — dirty before this session, NOT part of Phase 15)
next_action: Phase 15 is fully closed. Start the next roadmap phase: /gsd-discuss-phase 999.1 --auto (Phase 999.1 'MCP tool gaps — click heuristics'). Note: invoke GSD skills as /gsd-<name>; the /gsd:<name> namespace did not resolve in this environment.
context_notes: The full /gsd-discuss-phase 15 --auto pipeline ran clean end to end: discuss (assumptions mode, 2 external-research topics resolved) -> plan (3 plans, 3 sequential waves, plan-checker PASS) -> execute (3 waves, sequential on main tree, USE_WORKTREES=false) -> verify (passed) -> code-review (BLOCKER+4 warnings, all fixed inline + regression-tested) -> secure (15/15 closed). 218 trigger-family assertions green; INV-01 (MCP schemas byte-identical) and INV-04 (agent-loop.js byte-untouched) hold. The single biggest lesson: adversarial code review caught a real ReDoS DoS that all prior gates (plan-checker, executor self-check, verifier) missed because they trusted the plan's stated mitigation. Phases 16-19 also process untrusted page content — carry the structural-detector lesson forward.
